name: ndmanager
documentation:
# NDManager

[![Coverage
Status](https://coveralls.io/repos/github/nplinden/ndmanager/badge.svg?branch=develop)](https://coveralls.io/github/nplinden/ndmanager?branch=develop)

NDManager is a python package that helps you manage your nuclear data
files in a clean manner.

It provides an API and a CLI for creating and interacting with a
database of ENDF6 files, as well as processed data files and depletion
chain files in the OpenMC format.

In addition, it encapsulate the
[sandy](https://github.com/luca-fiorito-11/sandy) nuclear data sampling
tool to help you easily generate perturbed files in the OpenMC HDF5
format.

Full documentation is available
[here](https://ndmanager.readthedocs.io/en/develop/).

modules.name: ndmanager.env
documentation:
A module to retrieve user defined data to determine where to write the
libraries

modules.name: ndmanager.API.utils
documentation: Some utility functions

functions.name: ndmanager.API.utils.get_hdf5
documentation:
Get the path to processed nuclear data library in the OpenMC HDF5 format.

Args:
    libname (str): The name of the desired library
    sub (str): The name of the sublibrary (neutron, photon, thermal)
    nuclide (str): The name of the nuclide

Raises:
    ValueError: If the library does not exist
    ValueError: If the library does not contain the (sub, nuclide) couple

Returns:
    Path: The path to the HDF5 file

functions.name: ndmanager.API.utils.get_endf6
documentation:
Get the path to a ENDF6 tape stored in the NDManager database

Args:
    libname (str): The name of the desired evaluation
    sub (str): The name of the ENDF6 sublibrary
    nuclide (str): The name of the nuclide in the GNDS format

Raises:
    ValueError: The library does not exist
    ValueError: The sublibrary is not available for the library
    ValueError: The nuclide is not available in the sublibrary

Returns:
    pathlib.Path: The path to the library

functions.name: ndmanager.API.utils.list_endf6
documentation:
List the paths to ENDF6 evaluations necessary to build the cross-sections
and depletion chains.

Args:
    sublibrary (str): The sublibrary type (n, decay, nfpy).
    params (Dict[str, str]): The parameters in the form of a dictionnary.

Returns:
    Dict[str, Path]: A dictionnary that associates nuclide names to ENDF6 paths.

functions.name: ndmanager.API.utils.merge_neutron_file
documentation:
Merge two nuclear data file containing data for the same nuclide at
different temperatures.

Args:
    sourcepath: Path to the source data file. This file will not be modified
    targetpath: Path to the target data file. This file will be modified

Instantiate a Sampling object given a path to a yaml input file

Args:
    yaml_path (str): The path to the input file
    clean (bool): Delete existing sampled library if it exists

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.pendf_sampling.PendfSampling.sample_one_nuclide
documentation:
Manages the Sandy run for a given 3-tuple of nuclide name, cross-section
library name and covariance library name.

Args:
    tape (SampleTapes): A 3-tuple of nuclide name, cross-section library name,
                        a covariance library name
    processes (int): The number of processes to allocate

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.pendf_sampling.PendfSampling.run_sandy
documentation:
Run sandy to generate a perturbed library for a single nuclide

Args:
    xs_file (str): The path to the endf6 to perturb
    matrix_file (str): The path to the endf6 file containing the covariance matrices
    processes (int): The number of jobs to allocate
    ign (str): The group structure to use for the covariance matrix. Can be provided
               as an ign number from NJOY or using its name

A class to read nds input file and create perturbed nuclear data
from it.
This class perturbates 0K PENDF files and runs NJOY to perform
temperature treatment and conversion to the ACE format.

modules.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix
documentation: A class to generate covariance matrix libraries

classes.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix.CovMatrix

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix.CovMatrix.__init__
documentation:
Instantiate a covariance matrix using a nuclide name
and a dataframe containing covariances.

Args:
    nuclide (str): The name of the nuclide
    data (pd.DataFrame): The dataframe containing the covariances

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix.CovMatrix.from_tape
documentation:
Build a covariance matrix from an ENDF6 file, provided and group structure
using NJOY's nomenclature.

Args:
    path (str | Path): Path to an ENDF6 file
    ign (int, optional): The group structure using NJOY's ids. Defaults to 3.
    kind (int, optional): The kind of covariance matrix, only cross-sections
                          (kind=33) is available for now. Defaults to 33.

Raises:
    ValueError: If the requested matrix does not exist in the tape.

Returns:
    CovMatrix: The covariance matrix object.

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix.CovMatrix.export_to_hdf5
documentation:
Write the covariance matrix to an HDF5 format.

Args:
    path (str | Path): The path to the HDF5 file to write.

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix.CovMatrix.from_hdf5
documentation:
Reads an HDF5 file to build a covariance matrix

Args:
    path (str | Path): The path to the HDF5 file

Returns:
    CovMatrix: A CovMatrix object

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix.CovMatrix.submatrix
documentation:
Extract a submatrix from the covariance matrix

Args:
    mts (List[int]): The list of reactions to include in the matrix

Returns:
    CovMatrix: A new CovMatrix object

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix.CovMatrix.plot_block
documentation:
Plot the block corresponding the to covariances between reactions `mtleft`
and `mtright`. If an Axes object is provided the matrix will be drawn on the ax.
If a path is provided a matplotlib figure and ax will be created and the matrix
will be saved to a file.

Args:
    mtleft (int): The first reaction
    mtright (int): The second reaction
    path (str | Path, optional): The path to the plot file. Defaults to None.
    ax (plt.Axes, optional): A matplotlib ax to plot on. Defaults to None.

Raises:
    ValueError: If neither path of ax is provided
    ValueError: If both path and ax are provided

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix.CovMatrix.plot
documentation:
Plot the covariance matrix and save the result to a file

Args:
    path (str | Path): The path to save the image at

methods.name: ndmanager.API.sampling.covmatrix.CovMatrix.get_corr
documentation:
Extract correlation matrix. Copied from Sandy

Returns:
    CovMatrix: The correlation matrix
A class to generate covariance matrix libraries

methods.name: ndmanager.API.iaea.sublibrary.IAEASublibrary.download
documentation:
Download the all the tapes in the sublibrary to a directory specified by
`targetdir`.

Args:
    targetdir (str | Path): Path to the directory to write the tapes in
    style (str, optional): Style of the tape names. Defaults to "nuclide".
                           In {'nuclide', 'tsl', 'atom'}
    processes (int, optional): Number of download jobs to launch. Defaults to 1.

Raises:
    ValueError: If an unknown name style is passed to IAEASublibrary.download
    e: Raise errors raised by parallel download of nuclear data files

A class to manage a nuclear data sublibrary originating from the IAEA website.

Raises:

    e: Raise errors raised by parallel download of nuclear data files

Returns:
    IAEASublibrary: The sublibrary instance

modules.name: ndmanager.API.openmc
documentation: Set nuclear data paths for OpenMC

functions.name: ndmanager.API.openmc.set_xs
documentation:
Set openmc.config["cross_section"] value to the path to the
cross_sections.xml file of the desired library.

Args:
    libname (str): The name on the library, it must be installed beforehand.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: raised if the library is not installed.

functions.name: ndmanager.API.openmc.set_chain
documentation:
Set openmc.config["chain_file"] value to the path to the chain file of
the desired library.

Args:
    libname (str): The name on the library, it must be installed beforehand.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: raised if the library is not installed.

functions.name: ndmanager.API.openmc.set_nuclear_data
documentation:
Set openmc.config["chain_file"] and openmc.config["cross_sections"]
value to the corresponding paths for the desired library.

Args:
    libname (str): The name on the library, it must be installed beforehand.
    chain (str): The name on the library, it must be installed beforehand.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: raised if the library is not installed.

functions.name: ndmanager.API.openmc.check_nuclear_data
documentation:
Check that the OpenMC nuclear data library contains the desired nuclides.

Args:
    libname (str): The name of the library or a path to a cross_sections.xml file
    nuclides (str | List[str]): The nuclide of nuclides to check for

Raises:
    ValueError: _description_

functions.name: ndmanager.API.openmc.set_perturbed_xs
documentation:
Set openmc.config["cross_section"] value to the path to the
xml definition file of the desired sampled library.

Args:
    perturbed_library (str): The name on the perturbed library
    ismp (int): The perturbation ID to use.

Raises:
    FileNotFoundError: raised if the perturbed library is not available.

Initialize a perturbation list using the name of the perturbed library
and the kind of library (HDF5 or PENDF)

Args:
    perturbed_library (str): The name of the perturbed library
    kind (str): The method used to produce the library (HDF5 or PENDF)

modules.name: ndmanager.API.sha1
documentation: Some utility function to compute ENDF6 tape SHA1

functions.name: ndmanager.API.sha1.compute_file_sha1
documentation:
Compute the SHA1 value of a file given its path.

Args:
    Path to a file.

functions.name: ndmanager.API.sha1.compute_tape_sha1
documentation:
Compute the SHA1 hash of a tape stored in the NDManager database

Args:
    libname (str): The name of the desired evaluation
    sub (str): The name of the ENDF6 sublibrary
    nuclide (str): The name of the nuclide in the GNDS format. For TSL tapes,
                   the name of the tape

Returns:
    Dict[str, str]: A dictionary with the NDManager path of the tape as key and
                    SHA1 has as value

functions.name: ndmanager.API.sha1.compute_sublib_sha1
documentation:
Compute the SHA1 hash of all tapes in a sublibrary in the NDManager database

Args:
    libname (str): The name of the desired evaluation
    sub (str): The name of the ENDF6 sublibrary

Returns:
    Dict[str, str]: A dictionary with the NDManager path of the tapes as key and
                    SHA1 has as value

functions.name: ndmanager.API.sha1.compute_lib_sha1
documentation:
Compute the SHA1 hash of all tapes in a library in the NDManager database

Args:
    libname (str): The name of the desired evaluation

Returns:
    Dict[str, str]: A dictionary with the NDManager path of the tapes as key and
                    SHA1 has as value

functions.name: ndmanager.API.sha1.compute_sha1
documentation:
Compute the SHA1 hash of tapes in a library in the NDManager database.
If a sublibrary is specified, only tapes in that sublibrary will be computed.
If a nuclide is also specified, only the corresponding tape will be computed.

Args:
    libname (str): The name of the desired evaluation
    sub (str): The name of the ENDF6 sublibrary
    nuclide (str): The name of the nuclide in the GNDS format

Returns:
    Dict[str, str]: A dictionary with the NDManager path of the tapes as key and
                    SHA1 has as value

functions.name: ndmanager.API.sha1.check_tape_integrity
documentation:
Checks that the installed tape SHA1 sum matches the reference one

Args:
    libname (str): The name of the desired evaluation
    sub (str): The name of the ENDF6 sublibrary
    nuclide (str): The name of the nuclide in the GNDS format

Returns:
    bool: Wether the tape is valid

documentation:
Generate the requested covariance matrices

Args:

    ign (str): The group structure using NJOY ids

    processes (int): The number of jobs to allocate

Raises:

                     is False.

functions.name: ndmanager.CLI.sampler.cov.generate_one_matrix

Generate a covariance matrix given an ENDF6 library name, a nuclide name,
a group structure using NJOY's ids, and a directory to write in.

    nuclide (str): The name of the nuclide
    ign_value (int): The NJOY group structure id
    directory (Path): the directory to write in

modules.name: ndmanager.CLI.omcer.edit
documentation: Definition and parser for the `ndo sn301` command

functions.name: ndmanager.CLI.omcer.edit.overwrite_one_temp
documentation:
Substitute cross-section values for a given (nuclide, reaction, temperature) tuple
from a source HDF5 file in a target HDF5.

Args:
    source (h5py.File): The opened source file
    target (h5py.File): The opened target file
    nuclide (str): The name of the nuclide
    mt (int): The MT number of the reaction
    t (str): The name of the temperature node

functions.name: ndmanager.CLI.omcer.edit.overwrite
documentation:
Substitute cross-section values for a given (nuclide, reaction) couple
from a source HDF5 file in a target HDF5.

Args:
    nuclide (str): The name of the nuclide
    mt (int): The MT number of the reaction
    sourcefile (str): The path to the source file
    targetfile (str): The path to the target file

Raises:
    ValueError: If the temperatures available in the source and target file
                are different

functions.name: ndmanager.CLI.omcer.edit.find_negative
documentation:
Find negative cross sections in a nuclear data library HDF5 file

Args:
    matpath (str): Path to the HDF5 file
    mt (int): The MT number of the reaction

Returns:
    Dict[str, Dict[str, str | List[str]]]: Dictionnary of negative values

functions.name: ndmanager.CLI.omcer.edit.find_negative_in_lib
documentation:
Find negative cross sections in a nuclear data library xml file

Args:
    libpath (str): Path to a cross_sections.xml file
    mt (int): The MT number of the reaction

Returns:
    Dict[str, Dict[str, str | List[str]]]: Dictionnary of negative values

functions.name: ndmanager.CLI.omcer.edit.set_negative_to_zero
documentation:
Set negative cross-sections to zero in the file

Args:
    matpath (str): The path to the file
    mt (int): The MT number of the reaction

functions.name: ndmanager.CLI.omcer.edit.set_negative_to_zero_in_lib
documentation:
Set negative cross-sections to zero in all files in the nuclear data
library

Args:
    libpath (str): The path to the cross_sections.xml file
    mt (int): The MT number of the reaction

functions.name: ndmanager.CLI.omcer.edit.find_nuclide_in_lib
documentation:
Find the path to an HDF5 material file from a cross_sections.xml file

Args:
    libpath (str): Path to the cross_sections.xml file
    nuclide (str): Name of the desired nuclide

Returns:
    Path: Path to the material file

functions.name: ndmanager.CLI.omcer.edit.replace_negatives_in_lib
documentation:
Replace the negative cross-section values for a (nuclide, reaction)
couple in a cross_sections.xml defined library.

Args:
    target_path (str): Path to the target cross_sections.xml file
    source_paths (str): List of paths to the source cross_sections.xml files
    mt (int): The MT number of the reaction
    dryrun (bool, optional): Do not perform the substitution. Defaults to False.
    verbose (bool, optional): Additionnal log info. Defaults to True.

modules.name: ndmanager.format
documentation: Some functions to format printed output

functions.name: ndmanager.format.clear_line
documentation:
Move the print cursor up n lines.

Args:
    n (int, optional): Number of lines the cursor will be moved up. Defaults to 1.

functions.name: ndmanager.format.header
documentation:
Format a cool header with a title

Args:
    string (str): The title
    defcol (int, optional): Override column width default.

Returns:
    str: The formatted header

functions.name: ndmanager.format.footer
documentation:
Format a footer fitting the terminal size

Args:
    defcol (int, optional): Override column width default.

Returns:
    str: The formatted footer

functions.name: ndmanager.format.get_terminal_size
documentation:
Get the size of the current terminal window.
If such an information is not available (e.g. when ndmanager is run from
a Docker command or in pytest), it returns a default value that can be controlled
through the `defcol` and `defrow` parameters.

Args:
    defcol (int, optional): Default number of columns. Defaults to 150.
    defrow (int, optional): Default number of rows. Defaults to 80.

Returns:
    os.terminal_size: The size of the terminal
